summary: Core cleanup logic is thoroughly tested with good policy composition and edge case coverage. Workflow structure and E2E scenarios are well validated. However, there are critical gaps: the CLI argument parsing layer (parseArgs, parseIntStrict, parseNowOrThrow in src/cli.ts) is completely untested, as is the config file parsing layer (parseConfig, buildArgs in src/run-from-config.ts). These represent separate pieces of functionality per TDD requirements but have zero unit test coverage. Empty artifact list handling is not explicitly tested. The act-harness provides E2E safety but only tests happy paths; CLI errors would not surface until slow act runs. The design is clean with good fixtures and organization, but test granularity could be improved (e.g., formatPlanReport testing is embedded rather than standalone). Overall, regressions in cleanup core logic and workflow structure would be caught, but CLI/config parsing regressions would slip through unit tests and rely on E2E detection.
